title: Trichera × norica
taxonomy: Trichera × norica is classified as a hybrid species of flowering plant, resulting from the cross between Trichera alpestris and Trichera hirsuta. The classification of this plant falls within the family Asteraceae and order Asterales. It is native to Europe. The taxonomy is further categorized as a member of the subfamily Carduoideae. This classification reflects the plant's unique genetic makeup and evolutionary history.

intro: Trichera × norica, also known as Norica trichera, is a hybrid plant species within the Gentianaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activities, providing potential health benefits in the management of gastrointestinal disorders and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The bioactive constituents of Trichera × norica include gentiogenin, triterpenoids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trichera × norica is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried extract, and is commonly used to treat digestive issues and as a natural anti-inflammatory agent.
